testcase.name: AUDIT §1 — Cryptographic Integrity (NIST SP 800-57 / FIPS 140-3) > §1.1 — Key Derivation (PBKDF2-SHA256) > PROBE: determinism — same password+salt → same key
AUDIT §1 — Cryptographic Integrity (NIST SP 800-57 / FIPS 140-3) > §1.1 — Key Derivation (PBKDF2-SHA256) > PROBE: different passwords → different keys
AUDIT §1 — Cryptographic Integrity (NIST SP 800-57 / FIPS 140-3) > §1.1 — Key Derivation (PBKDF2-SHA256) > PROBE: different salts → different keys
AUDIT §1 — Cryptographic Integrity (NIST SP 800-57 / FIPS 140-3) > §1.1 — Key Derivation (PBKDF2-SHA256) > PROBE: key is exactly 256 bits (32 bytes) per FIPS 140-3
AUDIT §1 — Cryptographic Integrity (NIST SP 800-57 / FIPS 140-3) > §1.1 — Key Derivation (PBKDF2-SHA256) > PROBE: 100k iterations takes >10ms (brute-force resistance)
AUDIT §1 — Cryptographic Integrity (NIST SP 800-57 / FIPS 140-3) > §1.1 — Key Derivation (PBKDF2-SHA256) > PROBE: empty password does not crash (boundary)
AUDIT §1 — Cryptographic Integrity (NIST SP 800-57 / FIPS 140-3) > §1.2 — AES-256-GCM Encryption > PROBE: encrypt→decrypt is bit-exact
AUDIT §1 — Cryptographic Integrity (NIST SP 800-57 / FIPS 140-3) > §1.2 — AES-256-GCM Encryption > PROBE: ciphertext ≠ plaintext (not passthrough)
AUDIT §1 — Cryptographic Integrity (NIST SP 800-57 / FIPS 140-3) > §1.2 — AES-256-GCM Encryption > PROBE: different stream IDs → different ciphertexts (IV separation)
AUDIT §1 — Cryptographic Integrity (NIST SP 800-57 / FIPS 140-3) > §1.2 — AES-256-GCM Encryption > PROBE: tampered ciphertext → IntegrityError
AUDIT §1 — Cryptographic Integrity (NIST SP 800-57 / FIPS 140-3) > §1.2 — AES-256-GCM Encryption > PROBE: tampered auth tag → IntegrityError
AUDIT §1 — Cryptographic Integrity (NIST SP 800-57 / FIPS 140-3) > §1.2 — AES-256-GCM Encryption > PROBE: wrong key → IntegrityError
AUDIT §1 — Cryptographic Integrity (NIST SP 800-57 / FIPS 140-3) > §1.2 — AES-256-GCM Encryption > PROBE: tampered AAD → IntegrityError (AEAD guarantee)
AUDIT §1 — Cryptographic Integrity (NIST SP 800-57 / FIPS 140-3) > §1.2 — AES-256-GCM Encryption > PROBE: GCM tag is 128-bit (16 bytes)
AUDIT §1 — Cryptographic Integrity (NIST SP 800-57 / FIPS 140-3) > §1.2 — AES-256-GCM Encryption > PROBE: encrypting empty plaintext works
AUDIT §1 — Cryptographic Integrity (NIST SP 800-57 / FIPS 140-3) > §1.3 — Auth Verification (Timing-Safe) > PROBE: correct key verifies
AUDIT §1 — Cryptographic Integrity (NIST SP 800-57 / FIPS 140-3) > §1.3 — Auth Verification (Timing-Safe) > PROBE: wrong key fails
AUDIT §1 — Cryptographic Integrity (NIST SP 800-57 / FIPS 140-3) > §1.3 — Auth Verification (Timing-Safe) > PROBE: mismatched length → false (not crash)
AUDIT §1 — Cryptographic Integrity (NIST SP 800-57 / FIPS 140-3) > §1.3 — Auth Verification (Timing-Safe) > PROBE: 1-bit difference → still rejected
AUDIT §1 — Cryptographic Integrity (NIST SP 800-57 / FIPS 140-3) > §1.4 — Salt & Nonce Generation > PROBE: salt=16 bytes, fileNonce=12 bytes (GCM standard)
AUDIT §1 — Cryptographic Integrity (NIST SP 800-57 / FIPS 140-3) > §1.4 — Salt & Nonce Generation > PROBE: 1000 generations have zero collisions (CSPRNG)
AUDIT §2 — Data Integrity Chain (ISO 27001 A.8.24) > §2.1 — IntegrityChain (SHA-256) > PROBE: deterministic across instances
AUDIT §2 — Data Integrity Chain (ISO 27001 A.8.24) > §2.1 — IntegrityChain (SHA-256) > PROBE: order-dependent (A,B ≠ B,A)
AUDIT §2 — Data Integrity Chain (ISO 27001 A.8.24) > §2.1 — IntegrityChain (SHA-256) > PROBE: 10,000 updates — zero collisions, always 32 bytes
AUDIT §2 — Data Integrity Chain (ISO 27001 A.8.24) > §2.1 — IntegrityChain (SHA-256) > PROBE: empty data update does not break chain
AUDIT §2 — Data Integrity Chain (ISO 27001 A.8.24) > §2.2 — CRC32 > PROBE: detects every single-bit error in a 28-byte block
AUDIT §2 — Data Integrity Chain (ISO 27001 A.8.24) > §2.2 — CRC32 > PROBE: deterministic
AUDIT §2 — Data Integrity Chain (ISO 27001 A.8.24) > §2.2 — CRC32 > PROBE: empty data → valid number
AUDIT §3 — AuditChain Tamper Evidence (ISO 27001 A.8.15) > PROBE: 1000 entries → verify = 100% valid
AUDIT §3 — AuditChain Tamper Evidence (ISO 27001 A.8.15) > PROBE: tamper 1 entry payload → hash mismatch detected
AUDIT §3 — AuditChain Tamper Evidence (ISO 27001 A.8.15) > PROBE: deleted entry → chain continuity broken
AUDIT §3 — AuditChain Tamper Evidence (ISO 27001 A.8.15) > PROBE: sequence is strictly monotonic
AUDIT §3 — AuditChain Tamper Evidence (ISO 27001 A.8.15) > PROBE: payload >1024 bytes is SHA-256 hashed (no raw leak)
AUDIT §3 — AuditChain Tamper Evidence (ISO 27001 A.8.15) > PROBE: hash includes ALL fields (canonical)
AUDIT §3 — AuditChain Tamper Evidence (ISO 27001 A.8.15) > PROBE: 500 rapid concurrent appends → still valid chain
AUDIT §3 — AuditChain Tamper Evidence (ISO 27001 A.8.15) > PROBE: fsyncOnCommit=true — appends succeed and chain is valid
AUDIT §4 — WAL Crash Safety & Recovery > §4.1 — Binary WAL v2 > PROBE: 100 writes → recovery produces identical state
AUDIT §4 — WAL Crash Safety & Recovery > §4.1 — Binary WAL v2 > PROBE: CRC detects corruption, skips bad entry, recovers rest
testcase.system-err:
[WAL] CRC mismatch at offset 149. Skipping corrupted entry.
[WAL] CRC mismatch at offset 149. Skipping corrupted entry.

testcase.name: AUDIT §4 — WAL Crash Safety & Recovery > §4.1 — Binary WAL v2 > PROBE: DELETE operations survive recovery
AUDIT §4 — WAL Crash Safety & Recovery > §4.1 — Binary WAL v2 > PROBE: LSN continuity across restart (session1 → close → session2)
AUDIT §4 — WAL Crash Safety & Recovery > §4.2 — Binary vs JSONL WAL equivalence > PROBE: same ops → identical recovered state
AUDIT §4 — WAL Crash Safety & Recovery > §4.3 — Checkpoint & Compaction > PROBE: checkpoint survives compaction, all data recoverable
AUDIT §4 — WAL Crash Safety & Recovery > §4.3 — Checkpoint & Compaction > PROBE: corrupted checkpoint → falls back to full replay
testcase.system-err: [WAL] Invalid checkpoint entry. Falling back to previous checkpoint.

testcase.name: AUDIT §4 — WAL Crash Safety & Recovery > §4.4 — WAL edge cases (where it could silently fail) > PROBE: special chars in keys/values survive roundtrip
AUDIT §4 — WAL Crash Safety & Recovery > §4.4 — WAL edge cases (where it could silently fail) > PROBE: overwrite same key 100x → only last value survives
AUDIT §4 — WAL Crash Safety & Recovery > §4.4 — WAL edge cases (where it could silently fail) > PROBE: empty payload survives roundtrip
AUDIT §5 — Resilience Shell Failure Modes > PROBE: CLOSED → 5 failures in window → OPEN (fail-fast)
AUDIT §5 — Resilience Shell Failure Modes > PROBE: OPEN → cooldown → HALF_OPEN → probes succeed → CLOSED
AUDIT §5 — Resilience Shell Failure Modes > PROBE: backpressure rejects at highWaterMark
AUDIT §5 — Resilience Shell Failure Modes > PROBE: all errors carry metadata (attempts, circuitState, queueDepth)
AUDIT §5 — Resilience Shell Failure Modes > PROBE: HALF_OPEN failure → back to OPEN (no infinite retry loop)
AUDIT §6 — Supervisor Degradation & Recovery (ISO 22301) > PROBE: starts HEALTHY, transitions logged
AUDIT §6 — Supervisor Degradation & Recovery (ISO 22301) > PROBE: buffered writes have monotonic sequence IDs
AUDIT §6 — Supervisor Degradation & Recovery (ISO 22301) > PROBE: flush — WAL wins conflicts, non-conflicts applied
testcase.system-out: [Supervisor] Conflict: WAL wins for key="conflict", buffer write discarded.

testcase.name: AUDIT §6 — Supervisor Degradation & Recovery (ISO 22301) > PROBE: resetDegraded from non-DEGRADED state → returns false
AUDIT §6 — Supervisor Degradation & Recovery (ISO 22301) > PROBE: isDegraded initially false
AUDIT §7 — MemTable Boundary Conditions > PROBE: put/get/delete/overwrite cycle correct
AUDIT §7 — MemTable Boundary Conditions > PROBE: overwrite replaces ALL fields (no stale-field leakage)
AUDIT §7 — MemTable Boundary Conditions > PROBE: size estimation grows with data
AUDIT §7 — MemTable Boundary Conditions > PROBE: shouldFlush triggers at size threshold
AUDIT §7 — MemTable Boundary Conditions > PROBE: shouldFlush triggers at dirty threshold
AUDIT §7 — MemTable Boundary Conditions > PROBE: resetDirty clears dirty count
AUDIT §7 — MemTable Boundary Conditions > PROBE: scan prefix returns correct subset
AUDIT §7 — MemTable Boundary Conditions > PROBE: empty string key works
AUDIT §7 — MemTable Boundary Conditions > PROBE: unicode keys work
AUDIT §7 — MemTable Boundary Conditions > PROBE: delete non-existent key returns false, no crash
AUDIT §7 — MemTable Boundary Conditions > PROBE: get on empty table returns undefined
AUDIT §7 — MemTable Boundary Conditions > PROBE: accessCount increments on get
AUDIT §7 — MemTable Boundary Conditions > PROBE: 1000 concurrent put/get — no state corruption
AUDIT §8 — BanditRouter Determinism & Safety > PROBE: disabled by default → safe fallback
AUDIT §8 — BanditRouter Determinism & Safety > PROBE: seeded → fully deterministic
AUDIT §8 — BanditRouter Determinism & Safety > PROBE: recordOutcome updates alpha/beta correctly
AUDIT §8 — BanditRouter Determinism & Safety > PROBE: empty arms → safe fallback
AUDIT §8 — BanditRouter Determinism & Safety > PROBE: high-performing arm selected > 70% after 100 observations
AUDIT §8 — BanditRouter Determinism & Safety > PROBE: reset clears all arms
AUDIT §8 — BanditRouter Determinism & Safety > PROBE: disabled router ignores recordOutcome
AUDIT §9 — PromptDistiller Data Lifecycle (GDPR Art.5) > PROBE: store → retrieve from RAW tier
AUDIT §9 — PromptDistiller Data Lifecycle (GDPR Art.5) > PROBE: retention compresses old RAW data
AUDIT §9 — PromptDistiller Data Lifecycle (GDPR Art.5) > PROBE: distillation is irreversible — original content gone
AUDIT §9 — PromptDistiller Data Lifecycle (GDPR Art.5) > PROBE: tier stats are accurate
AUDIT §9 — PromptDistiller Data Lifecycle (GDPR Art.5) > PROBE: readOnly flag is initially false
AUDIT §9 — PromptDistiller Data Lifecycle (GDPR Art.5) > PROBE: legacy gzip files are readable after zstd migration
AUDIT §10 — File Locking & Concurrency > PROBE: exclusive lock — max 1 concurrent
AUDIT §10 — File Locking & Concurrency > PROBE: shared locks allow concurrent reads
AUDIT §10 — File Locking & Concurrency > PROBE: exclusive waiter blocks new shared (write-preferring)
AUDIT §10 — File Locking & Concurrency > PROBE: lock timeout fires
AUDIT §11 — Compression Core Roundtrip > PROBE: 100 snapshots, 5 items each — bit-exact roundtrip
AUDIT §11 — Compression Core Roundtrip > PROBE: encrypted roundtrip preserves data
AUDIT §11 — Compression Core Roundtrip > PROBE: wrong password → IntegrityError (not garbage)
AUDIT §11 — Compression Core Roundtrip > PROBE: truncated file → error thrown (not silent corruption)
AUDIT §11 — Compression Core Roundtrip > PROBE: single bit-flip → detected (integrity chain or CRC)
AUDIT §11 — Compression Core Roundtrip > PROBE: compression ratio > 1 for realistic data
AUDIT §11 — Compression Core Roundtrip > PROBE: determinism — same input → same output bytes
AUDIT §11 — Compression Core Roundtrip > PROBE: single snapshot roundtrip
AUDIT §11 — Compression Core Roundtrip > PROBE: 1000 snapshots stress test
AUDIT §11 — Compression Core Roundtrip > PROBE: empty snapshots list → does not crash
AUDIT §12 — Adversarial & Edge Cases (OWASP) > PROBE: massive items per snapshot (100 items)
AUDIT §12 — Adversarial & Edge Cases (OWASP) > PROBE: price=0 and quantity=0 survive roundtrip
AUDIT §12 — Adversarial & Edge Cases (OWASP) > PROBE: very large price values survive roundtrip
AUDIT §12 — Adversarial & Edge Cases (OWASP) > PROBE: negative price values survive roundtrip
AUDIT §12 — Adversarial & Edge Cases (OWASP) > PROBE: non-sequential timestamps survive
AUDIT §12 — Adversarial & Edge Cases (OWASP) > PROBE: random garbage bytes → error (not crash or hang)
AUDIT §12 — Adversarial & Edge Cases (OWASP) > PROBE: GICS magic bytes followed by garbage → error
AUDIT §13 — Determinism Guarantees > PROBE: IntegrityChain deterministic across instances
AUDIT §13 — Determinism Guarantees > PROBE: CRC32 is fully deterministic
AUDIT §13 — Determinism Guarantees > PROBE: encoder output is byte-identical across runs
AUDIT §14 — Cross-Module Integration Seams > PROBE: WAL + MemTable consistency after write cycle
AUDIT §14 — Cross-Module Integration Seams > PROBE: AuditChain records WAL operations faithfully
AUDIT §14 — Cross-Module Integration Seams > PROBE: Supervisor buffer → MemTable flush preserves data
AUDIT §14 — Cross-Module Integration Seams > PROBE: ResilienceShell wraps operations correctly
AUDIT §14 — Cross-Module Integration Seams > PROBE: Encoder output → Decoder → verify all stream sections
§15 — Compression Ratio Empirical Data > PROBE 112: Trending integers — 500 snapshots, 10 items → ratio > 25x
§15 — Compression Ratio Empirical Data > PROBE 113: Volatile integers — 1000 snapshots, 50 items → ratio > 55x
§15 — Compression Ratio Empirical Data > PROBE 114: Multi-item dense — 200 snapshots, 50 items → ratio > 40x
§15 — Compression Ratio Empirical Data > PROBE 115: Single snapshot — binary overhead dominates on tiny data
§15 — Compression Ratio Empirical Data > PROBE 116: Large dataset (60x target) — 2000 snapshots, 20 items → ratio > 50x
§15 — Compression Ratio Empirical Data > PROBE 117: Encrypted vs unencrypted overhead — bounded and proportional
testcase.system-out: [§15] Encryption overhead: plain=1655, encrypted=1802, delta=147 bytes, overhead=8.88%
